questionHtml:
Why are high voltages and low currents used when electricity is transmitted over long distances?

A.  Cables can be closer to the ground.

B.  Electrons have a greater drift speed.

C.  Energy losses are reduced.

D.  Resistance of the power lines is reduced.

meta.title: Why are high voltages and low currents used when electricity is transmitted over long distances? A. Cables can be closer to the ground. B. Electrons have a greater drift speed. C.
breadcrumbLabels: B. The particulate nature of matter
